title: Conditional formatting for status automation
excerpt:
Currently, the parent status can be automated to change when one of the sub-items changes to a specific status. I’d like to be able to be more granular.

posts.author: JMack (J)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently, the parent status can be automated to change when one of the sub-items changes to a specific status. I’d like to be able to be more granular.

Update the parent status to ‘x’ if the following sub-items have this status.

For example, “Change the (parent status to this) when (sub-item status changes to this) AND when (sub-item status changes to this) (continue for as many status as desired)”

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
J:

Currently, the parent status can be automated to change when one of the sub-items changes to a specific status. I’d like to be able to be more granular.

Update the parent status to ‘x’ if the following sub-items have this status.

For example, “Change the (parent status to this) when (sub-item status changes to this) AND when (sub-item status changes to this) (continue for as many status as desired)”

Hi @JMack

If you’re on Pro or Enterprise, you can partially achieve this using built-in workflows. For example, you can set up automations like “when a subitem status changes to something, change the parent item status to something.” I’ve attached some workflow examples below for reference.

image1242×584 47 KB

image1206×624 48 KB

However, this approach is limited since it triggers based on individual subitem changes and doesn’t support more granular conditions (like multiple subitems meeting specific statuses at the same time).

If you’re open to marketplace apps and need more advanced logic such as updating the parent only when all subitems reach certain statuses you can use an app like Subitem Automations, which provides more flexibility for these kinds of conditions.

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @JMack
This level of granularity isn’t supported by native automations yet, but you can achieve it reliably using Make Automation

Set up a scenario that triggers whenever a subitem status changes. Then Make will:

  • Fetch all subitems under the parent item

  • Check the status of each required subitem (or specific ones)

  • Verify that your full condition is met (e.g., Subitem A = Done AND Subitem B = Approved AND Subitem C = Complete)

  • Only then update the parent item status to “X”
